Hey, it's Andre and Sean. Today we are going to be talking about the relationship building series. This is a 10,000 foot view that you can see in the image. I'm going to just quickly give you an overview of what you're looking at and then we'll dive into the specifics. So, top of the screen, you can see the zoom in on the actual relationship building series and we have your series of emails to the left or right. And then if you look to the left below that, you'll see that that series is put into context. You'll see it says, you know, RBS on the left and there's that relationship building series and you can start to see how different, there can be a different journey for each person who goes through it. So, you can see with all the gray areas, the different potential ways somebody could, the gray arrows, different potential ways somebody could go through your system as you build it. So, you could have a relationship building series and then a soft bridge and then a story of power promotion. You could have others who see the relationship building series and maybe they go right to a soap opera series after that hard bridge and the product launch series. You could have a relationship building series that led to a series of value newsletters, which one of which perhaps leads to a soap opera series and they're just infinite ways that this can get combined. We wanted to put the zoom out so you can see that it fits in the beginning of the campaign of because it is a relationship building series always is the entry point for a prospect into our world. That's critically important. Every person who enters our world first sees relationship building series and it is not something that is designed to just be on the way to a sale. That's absolutely not what we mean when we talk about a relationship building series. There is a beginning to it and an end to it and everything that the entire experience is around interacting with the prospect through empathy and establishing credibility and authority but not trying to sell something. We are absolutely focusing on building a relationship with them first because that has a cascade of effects over time. Yeah, so I think let's stop by defining what a relationship building series is for because in the past this was a soap opera series and we've now changed it to, you know, so it's more clear of what this thing does and the whole point of this thing is, as Sean said, to quickly establish rapport and demonstrate empathy on authority and to earn trust and attention. And so one of the ways we do that is, you know, once somebody comes into this world and typically there's a world or there's some stuff that'll happen before somebody gets here, you know, this doesn't exist in isolation but for the purposes of this, once somebody gets that very first email and they then launched into this series of emails, we wanted to feel black and experience and even though there's no selling, when this thing is done really well, there is a feeling inside of subscribers that are experiencing this narrative which is pulling them forward. There's that sense that they want whatever this thing is or they want to hear from you. They just want more. It's weird. It's not just, they're not receiving, you know, just random isolated emails that's moving towards an eventual sale that they know is going to come. This is different and in fact, in some cases it can be slightly jarring in the sense that they think they can be sold and they're not because this thing is just so different. Yeah. I mean, that's such a great point too because we're also trained to expect, you know, like, oh, we're going to get an email. It's, hey, this is, you know, here's some stuff about me and then there's another email, how I can help you and it's just, we're so, we so expect when the second, third, fourth emails, we just feel like, okay, it's coming, right, even if it's just subconscious, we feel that. Yeah. And a relationship building series is great because it has a beginning and an end. It's narrative driven in between, so we're pulling people's attention forward, so they sense that, they sense that they're moving towards something. A relationship building series is a soap opera series. The difference is it's a very specific type of a very specific purpose. And that's why we called it out and we have a separate module in the art of email for it, because it's everything when you read the soap opera series module, you'll think your solicence just like the relationship building series. Exactly. You know, it's serialized narrative, there's a theme, there's a motion, you're pulling an audience forward with a narrative driven experience. The difference is that you can use the soap opera series is sort of like a Swiss army knife, you can use it for lots of different things. The relationship building series is always the entry points and very specific, critically important kind of soap opera series. And you will receive it the very next time that we send one out. And I think there are a couple of things I'll draw attention to here too. One in the same vein that you just did. And then one I would just want to mention before we get there. So when when we send people through a relationship building series, there are two things that we want to have happen. And one is obvious, and I think we're everyone's probably listening is assuming that, which is we want the right prospects to be drawn towards us. So there will be people who reply to your emails, like, Oh, this is amazing, what a great insight, or we get lots of those replies when you ask someone's going through it and their the lights are on and they're just wow, this is amazing, especially as they get toward the end. And we don't try to sell anything. And that's surprising and little squirt of dopamine and wow, like that was a different experience. But you're also going to find that there are a percentage of people who opt in and start to go through your relationship building series, and they're not your people and they make it very clear. They don't either stop paying attention to like, you know, sort of a gentle way of not engaging. You might find that you're getting feedback, replying to things disagreeing with you, depending on the nature of that series, whatever it is, but that's, that's okay. This is not something that's designed to please everybody to try to make lower quality prospects, a higher quality prospect, like you're not trying to take people who, you know, you can vacuum cleaner into your world, like I'll make everybody a good prospect. This is very much a filtering tool. You're pulling your the narrative driven experience pulls people toward you who are your best audience. And we'll naturally, when we say repel, there's degrees of repel, but it will naturally repel others. And that it's supposed to do both. So, you know, if you get, if you create a relationship building series, you think, wow, every once in a while, someone sends me an email and they didn't like it at all, should I change it? No, it means it's doing its job. Right. You should be filtering people out with your relationship building series passively and actively. I mean, it's, it's again, just choose your own adventure once you start to understand how the audience who is interacting with your content can indicate what is interesting to them right now, and continue to get more of that, and to, to refine more of that and to, to create their own path, you begin to understand the power of this. And for many of them that, that path is going to be much more passive, which is they're going to pay attention in spurts, where they'll be, they'll pay attention for part of the series, and then maybe not for a while, and then something in a value newsletter will catch their attention, and then they're back, and then perhaps they go through something else, but your, the system is responding to the audience and what the audience needs and directing their, their energy and attention, and it's doing that all the time. So it can be done very subtly, it can be done less subtly. This is completely up to you, but the RBS comes first. When somebody runs through this, depending on what you, what you want to have stacked next, they should always get tagged as, and then be, and then be knocked into your value newsletter. So whenever those go out, those people, you know, these people who have been through this will receive that. So yeah, and, but again, this is, this is just suggestions. This is not hard, hard rules. We don't have many hard rules, one of which is that, you know, you don't sell on an RBS, every single person has to run through an RBS, but outside of that, everything is just suggestions. We don't prescribe many things. I think, you know, imagining from the audience's perspective now to a couple of questions, you know, questions like, you know, how long should my relationship building series be, and how often do I send and other questions? And the answer is, you know, we like to refer to these as how long is a piece of string questions, right? There's, there's, who knows, right? It's everything is context dependent. So the way to think about a relationship building series is if you had a, an opportunity to create a really good prospect, like what would the things that you would want that person to know, so that by the time that, you know, if you could imagine, it's almost like a, a relationship building is almost like a mini course and how to be a great customer eventually. It's one way to think about it and not because we're trying to sell them right now, but because like for us, when, if someone enters our world, there, we have four or five, six, seven ideas that we, that are really central to what we believe, you know, one of which, for example, is a difference between infinite, an infinite approach to business and a finite approach to business. Like we want people to understand that when they come into our world. That's something that's, it's, it's critical to do that. So the, the relationship building series really is an opportunity, almost an educational opportunity that says, we don't say this explicitly, but what we're saying implicitly is if these, if you, these things that we believe that we hold dear, that are who we are and what we're about, resonate with you, then keep coming with us. And if they don't, that's okay. And this, we want to, this initial conversation, it's no different than having a conversation with somebody when you first meet them, that you'll know, based on what the conversations like this person and I, do we sort of see eye to eye on things? Do we have a lot in common? Do we not? That's what the relationship building series is. So it, it shouldn't be three emails. I think that's too short. I think we would kind of all agree that's too short. Five maybe is four or five, I would think is the low end. I think probably somewhere in the neighborhood of 10 is the upper end in general for most people. You know, I think that's probably the sweet spot between five and 10. Except it's what's necessary to really bring somebody from, they don't know who you are, what you're necessarily all about through understanding who you are, how you fit in their world, the value that you can give to them and sort of opening up and teasing about that there's, there's a lot more ahead. Like that relationship building series does all of that and it does it exceptionally well. And then individually and you know, frequency, you know, we hours is daily over the course of seven days. You could certainly do any combination of daily first and then longer after that. So you could, you know, I can certainly imagine a scenario or perhaps the first three days were once per day. And then maybe the fourth one is a couple of days later and then the fifth one is a couple of days after. You can really, it depends how it depends really for your particular use, what makes the most sense. And if that's daily over seven days, like hours, great, that makes sense. If it's five days daily, that's great too. There's no particular right answer. There's just the right answer for you. And one thing I'll mention before I hand it over to you to wrap things up for the day, Andre, is that when you were talking a moment ago about duration and, you know, different paths that someone can take through an experience with a business, I just wanted to mention the gentleman who reached out to us the other day and said, you know, hey, you can use me as an example. I've, I've, he had not been a customer and had been in your world and our world for, he said, five years and seven months, and he was about to become a customer. So you never know, right? I mean, we just don't, you know, there, that's what they're going to be people out there. But think about that. I mean, what a testament to somebody's attention to have number one to have actually figured that out, but also to realize someone has, there are people who will be there for, for months, they're meeting in your audience and people who are in our audience who have been there for months and even years who through the combination of the way the Lego bricks work together, something happens when it's right for them. And because they've been treated so well, and they've received so much value over that period of time, they've become a customer, they've, something happens in that moment because of how well they've been treated over time. And that moment is different for everybody. And the relationships building series is the thing that, that kicks it off and frames it for your everybody who enters your world. And it's critically important. All right, anything left to the wrap up before we close the door on this one? Yeah, I think I'll just reiterate a few things that the point of this thing isn't to move somebody through years quickly as possible. It is to give somebody an experience. This is, this is someone's typically someone's very first touch point. You know, maybe they've, they've been knocking about the website for a while, and then they decided to opt in. So they do have, have a small frame of reference. But for the most part, this is, this is the first email somebody receives. And, and you know, we are, we want to create an experience that's going to be memorable. So I think one of the, one of the ways to think about this is don't try and, you know, to think like, well, let me try and make this five days or one week and then construct emails to try and fit into that. You know, I think sometimes the other way around is better. It's like, this is the thing I want to share with them because it's important. It's going to frame our world. It's going to, it's going to orientate them to who I am, what I care about, and how we can help, you know, each other. And that once you've written that, it may then end up being nine days or six days. So our one that we wrote, we didn't decide, well, let's make it six days and then try and fit six things into it. We, we wrote our thing and it just happened to be six parts where she was seven parts. But as I said, part one ended up being page one on the website. And then that's how it just took shape. And then maybe there's a little bit of an intro in the beginning and maybe there's a bit of an, something at the end, and then it's a bit longer. And then it just happens to be a certain length. I think that's, that's perhaps a better way to think about this is, is, is, is, you know, think about this is how, what do you want to share, what's important to share with every single person that's coming into your world. What do they need to know? You know, it's, it's also about, you know, as, as Sean said, this is a filtering system and you want to filter people out. So it's, it's good to, to paint that picture right up front, that there's certain things that we don't do. We have red lines. And if that's something that, that you want, well, then you're in the wrong place. And, you know, and hit this hit the unsubscribe button. So yeah, I think that's, that's what I've got. That's a fun one. This is, you know, this is really the thing to start working on. Really, there are a couple of ways to go through the art of email. And one of them is to stop after this, this module and get to work. That's certainly a possibility. You may want to go through the entire course. And then sort of get a sense that certainly would be my preference. That's how I like to learn as I like to kind of get taken all the information and then decide where I want to start. In the art of email, it's a lot easier because the place to start is right here in the relationship building series. So you've already gone through module one, the introduction module two, the principles of serialized narrative. And now here we are in module three. If you want to begin to extract value for your own business immediately, then don't feel like you have to rush through to module four to find out what's next and on and on. Start working on your relationship building series now. It doesn't have to be perfect. It shouldn't be perfect. If you wait to make it perfect, it's not going to get finished. We know that we have PhDs in perfection at getting in the way. So that's one path through is just to go through this module, maybe the soap opera series module next to you. They're going to get that holistic view of the totality of what a soap opera series is in general in a relationship building series specifically. And then sit down and sketch it out. Just kind of get a sense of what's the narrative arc? Where are you taking them? What do you want them to know by the end? What do you want them to believe by the end about you, about themselves, about what's possible? Just sketch that out and start working on it. And you can also, there's a hybrid approach too. You can start working on that and you can continue to go through the course and continue to work on the various emails for your relationship building series or have a hard stop right now, get it done, and then move on to the next one. But this is really, perhaps, the only place in the art of email where there is a very specific thing that needs to happen first. This is, I think, the only place we're prescriptive where we say this is where it all begins. So thanks so much for your attention today and good luck with the relationship buildings series molecule.
